heading: Preliminary determinations
label: 1671b Preliminary determinations

children.heading: Determination by Commission of reasonable indication of injury
children.label: a Determination by Commission of reasonable indication of injury
i
children.text: is materially injured, or

children.label: ii
children.text: is threatened with material injury, or

children.label: B
children.text: the establishment of an industry in the United States is materially retarded,

children.label: i
children.text: within 45 days after the date on which the petition is filed, or

children.label: ii
children.text: if the time has been extended pursuant to , within 25 days after the date on which the Commission receives notice from the administering authority of initiation of the investigation, andsection 1671a(c)(1)(B) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of an investigation initiated under , within 45 days after the date on which the Commission receives notice from the administering authority that an investigation has been initiated under such section.section 1671a(a) of this title

children.heading: Preliminary determination by administering authority; expedited determinations; waiver of verification
children.label: b Preliminary determination by administering authority; expedited determinations; waiver of verification

children.text: Within 65 days after the date on which the administering authority initiates an investigation under , or an investigation is initiated under , but not before an affirmative determination by the Commission under subsection (a) of this section, the administering authority shall make a determination, based upon the information available to it at the time of the determination, of whether there is a reasonable basis to believe or suspect that a countervailable subsidy is being provided with respect to the subject merchandise.section 1671a(c) of this titlesection 1671a(a) of this title

Notwithstanding paragraph (1), when the petition is one subject to , the Administering Authority shall, taking into account the nature of the countervailable subsidy concerned, make the determination required by paragraph (1) on an expedited basis and within 65 days after the date on which the administering authority initiates an investigation under unless the provisions of subsection (c) of this section apply.section 1671a(b)(3) of this titlesection 1671a(c) of this title

Within 55 days after the initiation of an investigation the administering authority shall cause an official designated for such purpose to review the information concerning the case received during the first 50 days of the investigation, and, if there appears to be sufficient information available upon which the determination can reasonably be based, to disclose to the petitioner and any interested party, then a party to the proceedings that requests such disclosure, all available nonconfidential information and all other information which is disclosed pursuant to . Within 3 days (not counting Saturdays, Sundays, or legal public holidays) after such disclosure, the petitioner and each party which is an interested party described in subparagraph (C), (D), (E), (F), or (G) of to whom such disclosure was made may furnish to the administering authority an irrevocable written waiver of verification of the information received by the authority, and an agreement that it is willing to have a determination made on the basis of the record then available to the authority. If a timely waiver and agreement have been received from the petitioner and each party which is an interested party described in subparagraph (C), (D), (E), (F), or (G) of to whom the disclosure was made, and the authority finds that sufficient information is then available upon which the preliminary determination can reasonably be based, a preliminary determination shall be made on an expedited basis on the basis of the record established during the first 50 days after the investigation was initiated.section 1677f of this titlesection 1677(9) of this titlesection 1677(9) of this title

children.heading: .—General rule
children.label: A .—General rule
children.text: In making a determination under this subsection, the administering authority shall disregard any de minimis countervailable subsidy. For purposes of the preceding sentence, a countervailable subsidy is de minimis if the administering authority determines that the aggregate of the net countervailable subsidies is less than 1 percent ad valorem or the equivalent specific rate for the subject merchandise.

children.heading: .—Exception for developing countries
children.label: B .—Exception for developing countries
children.text: In the case of subject merchandise imported from a Subsidies Agreement country (other than a country to which subparagraph (C) applies) designated by the Trade Representative as a developing country in accordance with , a countervailable subsidy is de minimis if the administering authority determines that the aggregate of the net countervailable subsidies does not exceed 2 percent ad valorem or the equivalent specific rate for the subject merchandise.section 1677(36) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: a least developed country, as determined by the Trade Representative in accordance with , orsection 1677(36) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: a developing country with respect to which the Trade Representative has notified the administering authority that the country has eliminated its export subsidies on an expedited basis within the meaning of Article 27.11 of the Subsidies Agreement,

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: In the case of a country described in subparagraph (C)(i), the provisions of subparagraph (C) shall not apply after the date that is 8 years after the date the WTO Agreement enters into force.

children.label: I
children.text: the date that is 8 years after the date the WTO Agreement enters into force, or

children.label: II
children.text: the date on which the Trade Representative notifies the administering authority that such country is providing an export subsidy.

children.heading: .—Notification of article 8 violation
children.label: 5 .—Notification of article 8 violation
children.text: If the only subsidy under investigation is a subsidy with respect to which the administering authority received notice from the Trade Representative of a violation of Article 8 of the Subsidies Agreement, paragraph (1) shall be applied by substituting “60 days” for “65 days”.

children.heading: Extension of period in extraordinarily complicated cases
children.label: c Extension of period in extraordinarily complicated cases
A
children.text: the petitioner makes a timely request for an extension of the period within which the determination must be made under subsection (b), or

children.label: I
children.text: the number and complexity of the alleged countervailable subsidy practices;

children.label: II
children.text: the novelty of the issues presented;

children.label: III
children.text: the need to determine the extent to which particular countervailable subsidies are used by individual manufacturers, producers, and exporters; or

children.label: IV
children.text: the number of firms whose activities must be investigated; and

children.label: ii
children.text: additional time is necessary to make the preliminary determination,

children.heading: Notice of postponement
children.label: 2 Notice of postponement
children.text: The administering authority shall notify the parties to the investigation, not later than 20 days before the date on which the preliminary determination would otherwise be required under subsection (b), if it intends to postpone making the preliminary determination under paragraph (1). The notification shall include an explanation of the reasons for the postponement. Notice of the postponement shall be published in the Federal Register.

children.heading: Effect of determination by the administering authority
children.label: d Effect of determination by the administering authority
i
children.text: determine an estimated individual countervailable subsidy rate for each exporter and producer individually investigated, and, in accordance with , an estimated all-others rate for all exporters and producers not individually investigated and for new exporters and producers within the meaning of , orsection 1671d(c)(5) of this titlesection 1675(a)(2)(B) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: if applies, determine a single estimated country-wide subsidy rate, applicable to all exporters and producers, andsection 1677f–1(e)(2)(B) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: shall order the posting of a cash deposit, bond, or other security, as the administering authority deems appropriate, for each entry of the subject merchandise in an amount based on the estimated individual countervailable subsidy rate, the estimated all-others rate, or the estimated country-wide subsidy rate, whichever is applicable,

children.label: A
children.text: the date on which notice of the determination is published in the Federal Register, or

children.label: B
children.text: the date that is 60 days after the date on which notice of the determination to initiate the investigation is published in the Federal Register, and

shall make available to the Commission all information upon which its determination was based and which the Commission considers relevant to its injury determination, under such procedures as the administering authority and the Commission may establish to prevent disclosure, other than with the consent of the party providing it or under protective order, of any information to which confidential treatment has been given by the administering authority.

children.label: A
children.text: the alleged countervailable subsidy is inconsistent with the Subsidies Agreement, and

children.label: B
children.text: there have been massive imports of the subject merchandise over a relatively short period.

children.label: A
children.text: the date which is 90 days before the date on which the suspension of liquidation was first ordered, or

children.label: B
children.text: the date on which notice of the determination to initiate the investigation is published in the Federal Register.

children.heading: Notice of determination
children.label: f Notice of determination
children.text: Whenever the Commission or the administering authority makes a determination under this section, the Commission or the administering authority, as the case may be, shall notify the petitioner, and other parties to the investigation, and the Commission or the administering authority (whichever is appropriate) of its determination. The administering authority shall include with such notification the facts and conclusions on which its determination is based. Not later than 5 days after the date on which the determination is required to be made under subsection (a)(2), the Commission shall transmit to the administering authority the facts and conclusions on which its determination is based.

children.heading: Time period where upstream subsidization is involved
children.label: g Time period where upstream subsidization is involved

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Whenever the administering authority concludes prior to a preliminary determination under subsection (b), that there is a reasonable basis to believe or suspect that an upstream subsidy is being bestowed, the time period within which a preliminary determination must be made shall be extended to 250 days after the filing of a petition under or initiation of an investigation under (310 days in cases declared extraordinarily complicated under subsection (c)), if the administering authority concludes that such additional time is necessary to make the required determination concerning upstream subsidization.section 1671a(b) of this titlesection 1671a(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: in cases in which the preliminary determination was negative, the time period within which a final determination must be made shall be extended to 165 or 225 days, as appropriate, under ; orsection 1671d(a)(1) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: need not be made until the conclusion of the first annual review under of any eventual Countervailing Duty Order, or, at the option of the petitioner, orsection 1675 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: will be made in the investigation and the time period within which a final determination must be made shall be extended to 165 or 225 days, as appropriate, under , as appropriate, except that the suspension of liquidation ordered in the preliminary determination shall terminate at the end of 120 days from the date of publication of that determination and not be resumed unless and until the publication of a Countervailing Duty Order under .section 1671d(a)(1) of this title1section 1671e(a) of this titleSo in original. The words “as appropriate,” probably should not appear.1
